theme.rationale: High-contrast minimalist layout tailored for a fast-paced business environment (Car Wash). Uses stark whites, deep navy for authority, and signal blue for kinetic actions. Prioritizes data legibility and operational speed.

cards.classes: bg-white border border-slate-200 shadow-sm transition-all duration-200 hover:border-blue-200

buttons.outline: border border-slate-200 text-[#0A1128] hover:border-slate-300 hover:bg-slate-50 transition-colors

inputs.style: bg-white border border-slate-200 focus:border-[#0052FF] focus:ring-1 focus:ring-[#0052FF] transition-all

tables.header: bg-slate-50 text-xs uppercase tracking-wider text-slate-500 font-semibold border-b border-slate-200

badges.style: px-2.5 py-0.5 rounded-full text-xs font-semibold border

images.description: Abstract water drops on glass, use as a dark/moody overlay for the login page background to give a premium car wash feel.
Shiny blue car detail. Use for empty states (e.g., 'No cars currently waiting') with a reduced opacity.

testing.attributes: All interactive elements, forms, and key metric cards MUST include a 'data-testid' attribute using kebab-case (e.g., data-testid='submit-car-button', data-testid='metric-daily-revenue').

instructions_to_main_agent: Implement layout strictly using the 'Control Room' grid approach: dense, high data visibility, utilizing the full width of the screen.
Override default Shadcn UI radius variables. The user selected a 'Minimalist and professional (business style)'. Use sharp corners (radius: 0.5rem or 0).
Ensure the dashboard handles 3 roles effectively: Super Admin (all metrics), Cashier (payments), Employee (own cars). Use conditional rendering based on user role.
Strictly apply the status colors (waiting, washing, completed, paid) as defined in the 'colors.status' object across all tables and cards.
When building the login screen, use the 'login_background' image URL as a full-cover background with a heavy backdrop blur or dark overlay.
Do NOT use standard HTML input/select elements. Rely heavily on Shadcn UI Form, Input, Select, and Dialog components.
Charts must use the primary brand color (#0052FF) and deep navy (#0A1128) for data visualization. Avoid default multicolor charts.
All components must be written in standard JavaScript (.js/.jsx), not TypeScript.
